source.title: Nitrogen Bottle Explosion - BW Pioneer FPSO

context.region: Gulf of America, Walker Ridge Area Block 249
context.operator: Murphy Exploration & Production Company / BW Offshore USA Inc.

actions_taken: Crane operator shut valve to jet fuel pump immediately
Injured person reported to medic for evaluation
BSEE investigators conducted onsite follow-up investigation on April 29, 2025
Murphy sent ruptured bottle to independent third-party testing facility for metallurgical analysis
Murphy issued safety alert distributed companywide
Murphy began drafting new policies and procedures for nitrogen bottle filling operations
event.summary: On April 28, 2025, at approximately 10:30 a.m., a nitrogen bottle ruptured and exploded during refilling operations on the BW Pioneer FPSO in Walker Ridge Block 249. The bottle had been mistakenly assumed to be rated for 6,000 psi when it was only rated for 2,400 psi, and was recharged to more than double its maximum capacity. Additionally, an incorrect relief valve rated for 9,000 psi had been installed instead of the appropriate 2,400 psi valve. The explosion caused moderate to severe structural damage to the bottle rack, manifold, diesel fuel tank, and nearby equipment. A crane operator located approximately 18 feet away was struck by flying debris, sustaining minor bruises, a small neck lesion, and temporary hearing impairment. The root cause was over-pressurization due to equipment failure (incorrect relief valve) combined with human error (failure to identify correct pressure rating) and inadequate procedures and training.
recommendations: Implement formal written procedure for nitrogen bottle filling operations with detailed guidelines
Establish proper training program for personnel on cylinder markings, pressure ratings, and overpressure risks
Implement routine inspection and maintenance program for nitrogen bottles and relief devices
Ensure correct relief valves are installed matching cylinder pressure ratings
Update Job Safety Analysis to specify target fill pressure and identify overpressurization hazards
Properly identify and segregate nitrogen bottles by type (DOT vs UN-regulated) and pressure rating
Establish competency requirements for personnel interpreting cylinder markings

hazards.name: Explosive energy release from overpressurized vessel

consequences.name: Structural damage to bottle rack and surrounding equipment
Diesel fuel spill from damaged tank
Personnel injury from flying debris and blast effects

controls.name: Pressure relief devices (Rupture Discs)
controls.barrier_role: Prevent overpressurization by venting excess pressure
controls.lod_basis: Primary safety device to protect against overpressure

supporting_text: the relief valve installed on the bottle during a preventative maintenance program in December 2023 was rated for 9,000 psi instead of the appropriate 2,400 psi
Had the cylinders been fitted with the correct relief valve, the over-pressurization could have been avoided
the installation of a relief device with a burst pressure greater than the cylinder's design pressure contributed to the failure

controls.name: Pressure indicators on booster pump outlet and manifold
controls.barrier_role: Monitor pressure during filling operations to detect overpressurization
controls.lod_basis: Secondary monitoring to detect abnormal pressure conditions

supporting_text: Pressure indicators are installed on both the booster pump outlet and the manifold
At approximately 9:50 a.m., one operator checked the gauge and reported to have a pressure of 3,500 psi before leaving the area again
The two operators began filling the bottle and then left to complete other tasks, and periodically returned to check the pressure gauge

controls.name: Bottle rack with iron bars, ratchet straps, and steel door
controls.barrier_role: Contain and restrain bottle in case of rupture
controls.lod_basis: Physical containment to limit projectile hazard

supporting_text: The bottles being filled were positioned in a bottle rack secured by iron bars, ratchet straps, and a steel door that was bolted closed
The explosion caused structure damage to the bottle rack that it was placed in, the force also caused the other bottles in the rack to be thrown out
The door to the bottle rack was blown off by the explosion and contacted a diesel day tank for a pump skid approximately 8 feet away

controls.name: Manifold with isolation capability for individual bottles
controls.barrier_role: Isolate individual bottles during filling to prevent cross-pressurization
controls.lod_basis: Design feature to control pressure distribution

supporting_text: Each bottle is equipped with two pressure relief devices (Rupture Discs), one on the valve and one on the manifold
The bottles in the rack are connected via a manifold that allows operators to select individual bottles for filling while isolating the others

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for nitrogen bottle filling
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and control measures for safe operation
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to communicate hazards and procedures

supporting_text: Informal steps were listed on the JSA and work permits, but no official BW or Murphy procedure existed
the JSA did not specify the target pressure for charging, nor did it identify hazards related to over-pressurization
This oversight failed to highlight potential causes, such as the installation of an incorrect relief device

controls.name: Formal written procedure for nitrogen bottle filling operations
controls.barrier_role: Provide detailed guidelines and steps for safe nitrogen bottle filling
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to standardize operations and prevent errors

supporting_text: BW operators informed the BSEE investigators that there was no formal procedure in place for nitrogen bottle filling operations
Murphy does not have a formal written procedure with detailed guidelines on nitrogen bottle filling operations
A formal procedure as well as proper hazard identification would have allowed for detection of the incorrect valve that was installed

controls.name: Personnel training on nitrogen bottle identification and pressure ratings
controls.barrier_role: Ensure operators can correctly identify bottle type and pressure rating
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to build competency and prevent misidentification

supporting_text: There is no training program in place for the nitrogen filling operation
competency was a contributing factor, as there was no proper training in place for interpreting cylinder markings or understanding overpressure risks
Had personnel been properly trained, they would have been able to correctly identify the type of bottle and the pressure rating, as they relied solely on the valve indication

controls.name: Bottle identification and segregation by type and pressure rating
controls.barrier_role: Prevent selection of wrong bottle or installation of incorrect valve
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent misidentification at source

supporting_text: The facility had two types of nitrogen bottles on board: DOT-regulated, which contain internal threads to install the valve, and UN-regulated, which contain external threads to install the valve
These bottles had varying maximum pressure ratings, 2,400 psi and 6,000 psi, and were not properly identified or clearly segregated from one another, making pressure identification difficult
The nitrogen bottle that ruptured had been mistakenly assumed to be rated for 6,000 psi, when in fact it was only rated for 2,400 psi

controls.name: Routine inspection and maintenance program for nitrogen bottles
controls.barrier_role: Detect and correct unsafe conditions such as incorrect relief valves
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to identify and correct defects before operation

supporting_text: a routine inspection and maintenance program was not in place, allowing unsafe conditions to persist undetected
the relief valve installed on the bottle during a preventative maintenance program in December 2023 was rated for 9,000 psi instead of the appropriate 2,400 psi

controls.name: Barricades around nitrogen bottle filling area
controls.barrier_role: Restrict personnel access to hazardous area during filling operations
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to limit exposure to hazard

supporting_text: He was aware of the simultaneous operations occurring at the time and was also aware of the barricades surrounding the area
This area is heavily trafficked, serving as the main access route for the production unit

controls.name: Helicopter Landing Officer coordination with fuel pump operations
controls.barrier_role: Coordinate simultaneous operations to manage risk exposure
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to manage concurrent hazardous activities

supporting_text: He started the fuel pump when notified by the Helicopter Landing Officer, and approximately a minute later, the explosion occurred
At the time of the incident, helicopter operations were also underway, including hot refueling and passenger transfer

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
